evals.prompt: Create an entity model from the requirements in docs/requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: An entity model at docs/entity_model.md with a Mermaid erDiagram showing only entities and relationships (no attributes), and a 5-column attribute table for every entity. It should cover the library domain: members, authors, books, physical copies, and loans, with foreign keys linking books to authors, copies to books, and loans to both members and copies.

assertions.text: No attributes appear inside the Mermaid entity blocks
Every entity in the ER diagram has a ### section
Every attribute table has exactly the 5 required columns
Every foreign key references an entity that exists in the model
Every Validation Rules cell uses a value from the skill's reference table
Every entity implied by the requirements (member, author, book, physical copy, loan) is represented
Relationship cardinalities match the requirements: one author to many books, one book to many copies, one member to many loans, one copy to many loans
Each entity description is a single accurate sentence reflecting its role in the requirements

evals.prompt: hey can you draw up the data model / ERD for the library based on what's in docs/requirements.md? need the tables too
evals.expected_output: Same as library-standard: a complete docs/entity_model.md. Tests that a casual, less precise phrasing still yields a fully structured entity model rather than a loose sketch.

assertions.text: No attributes appear inside the Mermaid entity blocks
Every entity in the ER diagram has a ### section
Every attribute table has exactly the 5 required columns
Every foreign key references an entity that exists in the model
The output is a structured entity model document, not a loose prose description or a bare diagram
